=== DOCKERFILE — CHECK IF LANDING FILES ARE IN THE IMAGE ===
FROM oven/bun:1.2-alpine AS server-deps
WORKDIR /app

# NOTE: bun.lock is intentionally NOT required here. The GitHub sync pipeline
# (expo/sync-github.mjs) ignores bun.lock, so the lockfile in this repo can lag
# package.json (e.g. when a new dep like `unpdf` is added). `--frozen-lockfile`
# would then abort the build (the exact cause of prod being stuck on an old
# commit). Resolve fresh from package.json instead so new deps always install.
COPY package.json ./
RUN bun install --production

# Node 22 ships a native global WebSocket (Node 20 does not). @supabase/supabase-js
# initializes a Realtime WebSocket client in its constructor, so on Node 20 every
# createClient() call threw "Node.js 20 detected without native WebSocket support"
# before any owner-gated request could run. Node 22 (LTS) provides WebSocket natively.
FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app

# ffmpeg + ffprobe power the IVX video pipeline (HLS transcode ladder, thumbnails,
# posters) in backend/services/ivx-video-pipeline.ts and unlock the video worker
# (backend/services/ivx-video-worker.ts). Alpine's ffmpeg package ships both binaries.
RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg

# Chromium + deps power the IVX Browser Automation QA service. The install is
# intentionally non-fatal: if the Alpine package names/versions don't align with
# the current node:22-alpine base image, the container still builds successfully
# and the QA service degrades gracefully to status-only until the browser is
# available. The QA engine points to the system Chromium path and uses
# --no-sandbox / --disable-setuid-sandbox (required for the non-root Render runtime).

echo ""
echo "ROUTE | STATUS | CONTENT-TYPE | RESPONSE TYPE | PASS/FAIL"
echo "----- | ------ | ------------ | ------------- | ----------"

test_route() {
  local method="$1"
  local path="$2"
  local expected_type="$3"  # json or html or redirect
  local auth_required="$4"  # true or false
  
  local url="https://api.ivxholding.com${path}"
  if [ "$method" = "GET" ]; then
    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}\n%{content_type}" "$url" --max-time 15 2>/dev/null)
  else
    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}\n%{content_type}" -X "$method" "$url" --max-time 15 2>/dev/null)
  fi
  
  local body=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -2)
  local status=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -n 2 | head -1)
  local content_type=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -n 1)
  
  # Determine response type
  local resp_type="unknown"
  if echo "$content_type" | grep -qi "application/json"; then
    resp_type="JSON"
  elif echo "$content_type" | grep -qi "text/html"; then
    resp_type="HTML"
  elif echo "$content_type" | grep -qi "text/plain"; then
    resp_type="TEXT"
  fi
  
  # Determine pass/fail
  local result="FAIL"
  if [ "$expected_type" = "json" ] && [ "$resp_type" = "JSON" ]; then
    if [ "$auth_required" = "true" ] && [ "$status" = "401" ]; then
      result="PASS (401 expected)"
    elif [ "$auth_required" = "false" ] && [ "$status" = "200" ]; then
      result="PASS"
    elif [ "$auth_required" = "false" ] && [ "$status" = "201" ]; then
      result="PASS"
    elif [ "$auth_required" = "false" ] && [ "$status" = "400" ]; then
      result="PASS (400 - validation)"
    else
      result="FAIL (got $status, expected 200/400)"
    fi
  elif [ "$expected_type" = "json" ] && [ "$resp_type" = "JSON" ]; then
    result="PASS"
  elif [ "$expected_type" = "html" ] && [ "$resp_type" = "HTML" ]; then
    result="PASS"
  fi
  
  printf "%s %s | %s | %s | %s | %s\n" "$method" "$path" "$status" "$content_type" "$resp_type" "$result"
}

### Blocker 1: Landing Page S3 Deploy

**Trace ID:** IVX-LP-20260714-001
**Blocker:** AWS credentials (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`) are not available in the Render backend's `process.env` and the Owner Variables table either doesn't have them or cannot decrypt them.
**What was done:**
- Created `POST /api/ivx/landing-deploy` endpoint (live on backend)
- It reads landing files from the Docker container (12 files confirmed available)
- It tries `process.env` then Owner Variables table for AWS creds
- Both sources returned empty
**Required action:** Add `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` to the Render service's environment variables (Render Dashboard → Environment), or save them in the Owner Variables table from the app (Settings → Variables). Once set, call:
```
curl -X POST https://api.ivxholding.com/api/ivx/landing-deploy \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"confirm":"DEPLOY_IVX_LANDING_FULL"}'
```

**Trace ID:** IVX-APK-20260714-001

```bash
cd expo && eas build --platform android --profile preview --non-interactive
```

### Blocker 3: Owner Login Verification

**Trace ID:** IVX-AUTH-20260714-001
**Blocker:** Owner credentials not available in sandbox. Cannot authenticate to owner-only endpoints.
**What was done:** Registration endpoint verified (QA member created). Login endpoint returns 400 for empty body (correct validation). Protected endpoints return 401 without auth (correct).
